latin: discipuli novi in Atrium Magnum intraverunt, ubi candelae innumerae in aere pendebant. in mensa ante omnes petasus vetus et sordidus positus erat. petasus Distribuens erat: in capite cuiusque discipuli positus, domum eius pronuntiabat. Ronaldus in Gryffindorem missus est. cum petasus in capite Harrii positus esset, vox parva susurravit: 'difficile est — sed ubi te ponam? GRYFFINDOR!' tota mensa Gryffindorensium clamavit et plausit.

questions.prompt: What was floating in the air of the Great Hall?
How did the Sorting Hat decide where to place students?
questions.target: It was placed on each student's head and announced their house
choices: It was placed on each student's head and announced their house

questions.prompt: What house was Harry sorted into?
How did the Gryffindor table react?

translation: The new students entered the Great Hall, where countless candles were hanging in the air. On a table before everyone an old and shabby hat had been placed. It was the Sorting Hat: placed on each student's head, it announced their house. Ronald was sent to Gryffindor. When the hat was placed on Harry's head, a small voice whispered: 'It is difficult — but where shall I put you? GRYFFINDOR!' The whole Gryffindor table shouted and applauded.
